msDesc.head: Wall painting of the martyrdom of St Edmund, at the eastern end of the northern wall of the nave
Tomb of Thomas Knolles
Great west door
Memorial brass for Robert Hylton, yeoman of guard to Henry VIII, 1523
Memorial brass of John Pynnoke, first priest of the chantry founded by Sir John Norbury, 1521
Memorial brass of Sir William Martyn, 1524
Memorial brass of a chrisom
Memorial brass effigy in civilian dress with foot inscription, c. 1505
Incised slab to William Farnham (d. 1548)
Alabaster slab for Ralph Wodford esq (d. 1498)
Memorial brass for a priest in mass vestments, c. 1430
Window glazed at expense of Robert Ferrour, mayor of Norwich in 1526 and 1536, and Robert Jannys, mayor of Norwich in 1517 and 1524
Stained glass roundel with nimbed dove descending in a glory with a scroll; early sixteenth century?
Dedicatory inscription in the east window of the south (Lady) chapel; probably fifteenth century
Eastern window of the south aisle over a portrait of a person praying
Wooden tomb of Sir John Savile (d. 1505) and his two wives Alice (Vernon) (d. 1493) and Elizabeth (Paston) (1529)
Tomb of Alice Cresacre
Palimpsest memorial brass appropriated for Thomas Sparrow (d. 1513); effigy of a man in civilian dress with a three-line Latin foot inscription and an English couplet in prayer scroll
Memorial brass of Brian Rouclyff (d. 1494) and his wife Joanne
Memorial brass of Sir Richard Wryght (1515)
Memorial brass of William Clarrell and his wife Margaret, 1460 (?)
Memorial brass of Thomas Middleton of Kirkby Overblow, brother of Nicholas Midleton and his wife Joan (daughter of Sir William Plumpton)
Memorial brass (1505) (?)

A broad hoop engraved on the outer side with flowers and leaves. Within this are lozenge-shaped panels, engraved with the Trinity, the Virgin and Child, and a bearded male saint. Excavated at the Benedictine nunnery at Godstow, Oxfordshire, before 1868.
Finger ring with black letter inscription on the inner surface
Carved memorial in the porch; late fifteenth century
Carved memorial

adminInfo.note: Transcription in BL, MS Add. 36295, f.17r [MS owned by John Anstis, antiquarian (1669 –1744)]

msDesc.head: Memorial brass of Sir Philip Tilney (d. 1453), husband of Isabel, daughter and coheiress of Sir Edmund Thorp of Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk
Small incised slab
Memorial brass of John Toddenham, c. 1450
Stained glass panel; probably early sixteenth century
Stained glass panel; probably early sixteenth century
Stained glass panel; probably early sixteenth century
Stained glass panel; probably early sixteenth century
The third pier of the south arcade
Inscription in wood on the south side of the chancel
